TextENGNew: ``Ahh, that's probably Kasai-san.@k@vS09/03/170300094. He's Shion's watchdog.``
``Her watchdog?``
``Ahahaha, yeah!@k@vS09/03/170300096. Shion has a tendency to do outrageous things compared to me,@k@vS09/03/170300097. so she needs someone keeping an eye on her.``
``You two are pretty neck and neck on that front, if you ask me.``
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Keiichi-kun lobbed that remark in, and just like that, he and Mii-chan were off horsing around as usual.
From what Mii-chan said, this Kasai-san was something of a local power broker in Okinomiya.
...In other words, not the kind of person I could just walk up to and meet.
But if he was Shii-chan's attendant, I might be able to find a way through her.
The problem was that I had no real connection to Shii-chan either.
We'd joke around whenever she happened to pop up in Hinamizawa, but I'd never once reached out to her on my own.@k I had no idea how to arrange a meeting.
And even if I could meet him, there was no guarantee he'd help.
...Even at the coffee shop, he'd only talked because Shii-chan had pushed him. He seemed like someone who made a point of not speaking lightly about others.
...While I was fumbling around with these roundabout approaches, things could spiral further into the mud—and become unsalvageable.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: From what I'd overheard, they'd been talking about the man making his entrance soon.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: That would mean the finale of their scheme.@k ...They were going to take everything.
Maybe I simply wasn't feeling enough urgency... but the catastrophe might already be right around the corner.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Even now, here I sat, peacefully attending class,@k ...but what if, at this very moment, those men had stormed into our house to threaten Dad and seize his bankbooks?
...Once anxiety took hold, it bred more anxiety. I felt a crushing pressure bearing down on me, as if the ceiling were about to collapse.
``Man... it's so peaceful it's boring.``
Keiichi-kun spoke to me between yawns.
``Is that so?@k@vS09/02/170200289. I'd love a life that was peaceful enough to be boring...``
``Not me.@k@vS09/01/170100216. I wouldn't mind if Martians invaded, say, not every week, but at least once a month or so.``
``Monthly tests don't count, though.``
``If Martians really did attack, destroying the world, burning Hinamizawa to the ground—would that cure your boredom, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS09/02/170200291. ...Would it?``
``Hey, I didn't mean it like that.@k@vS09/01/170100219. I was just saying a little excitement would be nice, because I feel like I'm going to dissolve from boredom.``
...He had no idea what I was going through, of course.
But his thoughtless words still irritated me sometimes.
What could possibly be wrong with a peaceful, boring life where you never doubt that today and tomorrow will be just like yesterday?
I knew that peaceful, boring days could shatter without warning.
I knew that on any ordinary day, your mother might suddenly announce she wants a divorce.
I knew that your father might suddenly take a lover, leaving you with no place in your own home.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: And yet, the days repeated themselves with maddening monotony.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...That was precisely why I wanted to live each day to the fullest—so I'd be ready whenever the world fell apart.
``I'm envious of you, Rena. You always seem so happy.@k@vS09/01/170100221. Is that a skill? I kind of wish I had it.``
``...I don't really know what to say to that.``
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ``Is there some trick to it? Living every day not bored, but happy?@k@vS09/01/170100223. If there is, teach me! I want to learn!``
``Ahahahaha, it's easy.@k@vS09/02/170200294. ...You just have to notice.``
``Notice what?``
You'd never be able to notice it, Keiichi-kun.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: And honestly... not noticing probably means you're living a truly happy life.
``You just have to notice@k@vS09/02/170200296. that happy days don't last forever.``
The usual walk home, the three of us together.
At the fork where Mii-chan split off, I chased after her as she waved goodbye.
``Mii-chaaaan...!``
``Hm? What?``
Mii-chan looked herself over, apparently thinking she'd dropped something.

``Um, ahaha, I almost forgot.@k@vS09/02/170200299. Actually, I ran into Shii-chan and Kasai-san at a coffee shop yesterday.@k@vS09/02/170200300. And I picked up something Kasai-san dropped while he was there.``
The lost item was a complete fabrication, of course.
If I actually managed to meet him, I'd just show him some random thing, apologize for the mix-up, and be done with it.
``Oh! Well, thanks for that.@k@vS09/03/170300100. I'll get it to him.``
``Ah—actually, I'd like to return it to him in person.``
Mii-chan blinked in surprise.
Naturally.@k I had virtually no connection to Kasai-san.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: By any measure, it was a strange request.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ``Well, I don't mind, but why?``
``H-Hau...@k@vS09/02/170200303. ...His sunglasses and that little beard?@k@vS09/02/170200304. They were kinda kyute...☆@k@vS09/02/170200305. Hauu~``
Could I bluff my way through this with Kyute Mode?@k ...I tried it.
Mii-chan isn't the type to overthink things, so it would probably work.
``Ahahaha!@k@vS09/03/170300104. Just don't pluck out his mustache and take it home with you!``
``So, um, do you think I could meet him...?``
``Hmm... hard to say. I have no idea whether he's busy or free.``
``I'll ask him if he has any reason to come to Hinamizawa soon.``
An indefinite "sometime in the future" wasn't going to cut it.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...I didn't need it to be today, but tomorrow or the day after would be ideal...
``Hey, what are you two doing over there?``
``Hmm?@k@vS09/03/170300108. Nfufufu!@k@vS09/03/170300109. It seems Rena has fallen for someone new~``
``N-No! That's not it! Hauuu!!``
changeReason: New translation adds clarifying phrase 'That's not it' not present in original

TextENGNew: ``What, really? That sounds interesting!@k@vS09/01/170100227. Let me in on it!``
I didn't want this to get brushed off, so I whispered in Mii-chan's ear: "I should return it soon—I'm sure Kasai-san is looking for it."
Mii-chan chirped "Roger, roger!" and walked away waving.
...I hadn't thought at all about what to say to Kasai-san if I managed to meet him, or how to ask for his help.
But he seemed to hold a position above Rina-san's group.@k If he agreed to help, it would be an enormous relief.
Still, there was a real chance he'd refuse, saying he made it a policy not to meddle in private affairs.@k He could just dismiss the whole thing as a father-daughter love triangle.
``See ya, Rena.@k@vS09/01/170100229. See you tomorrow!``
``Yeah, see you tomorrow!``
After parting with Keiichi-kun, I walked the rest of the way home alone.
Without the chatty Keiichi-kun, the sudden quiet seemed to sharpen my thinking.
...What I needed right now was quiet time to think about what lay ahead.
I shouldn't be naive enough to think Kasai-san would neatly resolve everything.
There was a good chance he'd decline, and I'd need to think of something else.
...In fact, I should probably plan for that as the default.
...That triangle—father, daughter, and another woman—kept nagging at me. Because I knew one just like it, intimately.
changeReason: Changed from 'love triangle' to 'triangle' (father-daughter-woman), removing romantic implication

TextENGNew: Yes—@k it was Satoshi-kun's family.
changeReason: Old says something 'happened to Satoshi-kun' but Japanese says it was about 'Satoshi-kun's family.'

TextENGNew: Last year, Satoshi-kun had faced the same thing.
In a triangle of brother, sister, and aunt, he'd fought to protect his sister.
The shape was the same as mine—I was fighting to protect my father.
Satoshi-kun fought alone.
Because no one helped him.
I hadn't done anything beyond offering words of sympathy and pity.
Because I didn't think there was anything I could do.
I'd felt sorry for him, yet never lifted a finger.
All I'd done was offer irresponsible encouragement and stoke him with words sharp enough to cut.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...I was only now beginning to understand how deeply that must have wounded him, thanks to the sting of Keiichi-kun's thoughtless words.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I was now walking the same path Satoshi-kun had walked last year.
...A strange sense of déjà vu.@k An eerie repetition.
...Ahaha. Then this year... am I the one who gets "spirited away"?
It's fine. I'm not thinking about running away from Hinamizawa...
...Am I, really?
I hadn't thought about running from Hinamizawa. But hadn't I thought about running from home, over and over?
Wasn't that why I'd built myself a refuge in the junk heaps?
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: It just happened to be within Hinamizawa.
If that junk heap had been somewhere outside Hinamizawa...@k then fleeing there would be the same as fleeing Hinamizawa.
...Ahahahaha... I shouldn't think like that.@k Oyashiro-sama would be angry...
Oyashiro-sama is... truly frightening.
...Flickering... flickering...
...No good. Every time I tried to remember that period, my consciousness went hazy.
The nasty memory of being forced to swallow those garishly colored capsules came flooding back...!
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: And with it, flickering clouds spread through my mind...@k Flickering, flickering, flickering, flickering...@k ...Don't force yourself to remember. Don't force it...
Ah... no, no...@k ...I couldn't stop the flickering in my head...
This feeling was dangerous.@k ...I had to fill my heart with something else...
Right—I didn't have time to let my head fill with abstract flickering.
...I should think about what to do if Kasai-san refused to help.@k I had to protect Dad from Rina-san and her people.@k ...And why was I still calling that woman "-san"?
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Rina is Rina. Rina, Rina, Rina.@k A "bad person" who seduced my father and sought to tear our family apart.@k Bad people are bad simply by existing.

A fundamentally different kind of being from the ninety percent who aren't bad.@k If you tolerate them vaguely, everything around them festers and swells.@k Like one rotten tangerine in a box—it spoils every tangerine around it.
...How did Satoshi-kun resolve his triangle last year? Ah, the flickering again... flickering, flickering...@k Flickering, flickering, flickering, flickering...
Just as the swirling flickers in my head were making me dizzy, my house came into view.
...I could also see Rina-san's scooter parked by the gate.
The instant I saw that scooter, the flickering subsided, and I came back to myself.
Rina-san had stayed the night last night after all.
She'd mentioned having a shift at her club this evening, so she'd probably be leaving soon.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: But even for that short while, I didn't want to be around her.
I called out "I'm home!" as I burst through the front door, pretended a friend was waiting for me outside, and scrambled right back out.
Rina-san caught me before I could escape.
``Oh, welcome back, Reina-chan.``
``Ah, hello...``
This was the first time Rina-san's smile had looked this revolting to me.
Of course it did.@k I now knew what she was scheming behind that smile.
``Your father and I went all the way to Gogura for lunch today.@k@vS09/20/172100082. There's a restaurant there with the most delicious keema curry.``
Dad poked his head out from the living room, saying it was so good we should all go next time.
..."All of us"—did that mean just me and Dad, or did it include her?@k More to the point, I hated hearing Rina-san say "your father."

``And we bought some retort curry as a souvenir.@k@vS09/20/172100084. Make sure you try it later.@k@vS09/20/172100085. It's really good!@k@vS09/20/172100086. Have you been to that restaurant, Reina-chan?``
I did know the keema curry place she was talking about.
It was a newly opened restaurant I'd seen in a magazine.
Very good food, but very expensive.@k The retort curry "souvenir" alone probably cost as much as several normal Ryuugu family dinners.
...Knowing what was in the Ryuugu family bankbooks, I couldn't stay calm about even this.
``I'm looking forward to it.@k@vS09/02/170200311. Thank you, Rina-san.``
``So, Reina-chan.@k@vS09/20/172100088. Your father and I would like to talk to you about something.``
``I'm sorry!@k@vS09/02/170200313. A friend is waiting for me, so I need to run!@k@vS09/02/170200314. Hau~!@k@vS09/02/170200315. I'm going treasure hunting at the dump again☆!``
``Ahahahaha!@k@vS09/20/172100090. You really do love treasure hunting, Reina-chan.@k@vS09/20/172100091. Maybe I should tag along sometime.``
``Well, excuse me—I've got to go!``
Every time Rina-san so much as moved, a wave of her perfume assaulted my nostrils.
I'd reached my limit. I couldn't stay there another second without grimacing.
I burst out the front door and ran frantically, as if fleeing the lingering scent that seemed to cling even outside.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I could feel it—the rubber band called Rena, stretched taut to its breaking point.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: If something gave, it would snap with devastating force. I knew that.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Why did I have to feel this way in my own home?
Wasn't your home supposed to be the one place where you could truly let your guard down?@k I ran.@k I ran from that place.@k This house was no longer somewhere I could exist, so I ran and ran.
It wasn't running toward something. It was fleeing.
Fleeing to a place where I was allowed to exist.
...Hadn't I spent all day steeling myself to fight that woman?
Hadn't I resolved to confront her directly, or with Dad present?
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: And yet, the moment I saw her false smile, a visceral revulsion raced up my spine.
It was like idly lifting a stone and finding it carpeted with pill bugs underneath—an instinctive recoil, slamming the stone back down.
I bolted from the house.
Did I want to fight, or did I want to run?
Was I simply choosing to pass the time in blissful ignorance at my junk-heap hideout, trusting that tomorrow would be the same as today?
But I couldn't keep doing that.
There was no telling when they'd make their move!
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I couldn't afford to waste time. I knew that in my head!
I knew it, but... I wanted to run. I couldn't stop myself from running. And I ran!!
...I already knew how impossibly soft the hideout mattress felt at times like these.
The cries of the higurashi were deeply soothing.
Their song was so gentle.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...Unlike music, they imposed no particular emotion.
...They simply, gently, seemed to tell me: just be yourself, as you are.
Because it was so quiet here, I immediately noticed the crude purr of an engine drawing closer.
This place must have been bustling during the dam conflict, but now it was a forgotten corner where hardly anyone set foot.
...So anyone deliberately passing through here was a rarity.
I sat atop the roof of the junked car, not bothering to turn around, waiting for the boorish intruder to pass on through.
But the engine stopped.
...I'm not stupid.@k That alone told me who it was.
The voice calling out from a distance—I ignored it the first time.
...But the person came close enough that ignoring them became impossible, and called again. I turned and answered.
``...Rina-san.``
``Reina-chan, are you hard of hearing?``
``Sorry, I think the wind was blowing the wrong way and I couldn't hear you.@k@vS09/02/170200319. Ahaha.``
She seemed to accept that flimsy excuse.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Rina-san laughed and said, "Yeah, that happens."
``Alone?@k@vS09/20/172100094. Where's your friend?``
``I'm playing by myself today.``
``Hmm.@k@vS09/20/172100096. That's pretty unusual for a girl your age.``
Rina-san stretched with an extended groan and surveyed the vast expanse of junk sprawling across the slope.

``Man, they really went all out dumping scrap here.@k@vS09/20/172100098. You know what?@k@vS09/20/172100099. There used to be a place near Hirasaka Second Block that was piled high with nothing but tireless cars.``
I had zero interest in Rina-san's reminiscing, but I nodded along politely.
This road wasn't on her way back to Okinomiya.
She must have known I'd be here and made a detour.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...What did she want?
She was clearly trying to make conversation with me.
...Had my rather blatant avoidance earlier bothered her? Was she here to smooth things over?
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Whatever her intentions with Dad, antagonizing his actual daughter wouldn't serve her interests.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: In that sense, it wasn't strange at all.
I nodded along to stories I didn't want to hear, thinking only: please just leave.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...Shouldn't I be fighting?
changeReason: Specific target 'against her' removed; JPN 戦わなくていいの has no specific target

TextENGNew: Hadn't I resolved to fight her?
changeReason: Specific 'against her' removed from the translation

TextENGNew: I'd learned the truth about this woman at that coffee shop in Okinomiya.
I'd seen inside the safe and learned how she was bleeding Dad dry.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...I know who you are.@k Please leave my father.
How easy it would be if those two sentences could settle everything.@k ...She'd almost certainly deny it.
No matter how much I told her what I'd overheard at the shop, I had no hard evidence.
If she claimed she'd only done it because that violent man Teppei forced her, I'd have nothing more to say.
...But I knew this woman was a "bad person"!
I knew that if I kept wasting time, something terrible would inevitably happen in the not-so-distant future...!
An impulsive emotion was building inside me, little by little.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...But I didn't know what shape to give it.
And as that feeling swelled, her perfume became unbearable...
...Wanting to put distance between us, I leapt down the scrap-strewn slope.
Down and down.@k Descending the slope of the junk heap.
When I looked back, she was following, saying something as she picked her way down.@k ...Was she really going to follow me?
Desperate to shake her, I ran faster down the slope, circled behind the heaps, and arrived at my hideout.
...Pointless flight.
She'd catch up eventually.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: All I was doing was leading that woman straight to the hideout no one was supposed to know about.
At the very least, I didn't want her to see this precious place of mine.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...I'd just decided to leave when she appeared.
``...Wow.@k@vS09/20/172100101. This is pretty cool. Like a secret base!``
``Ahahaha.@k@vS09/02/170200322. This is my secret hideout, just for me.@k@vS09/02/170200323. Nobody comes here, and nobody can HEAR anything.``
...A strange dissonance in my own words.
``Reina-chan's own secret hideout?@k@vS09/20/172100103. Ahahahaha!@k@vS09/20/172100104. I'm honored to be shown such a special place.``
She seemed to take my sharing of this secret as a sign of closeness, and looked pleased with herself.
She found the station wagon hideout, peered inside through the window, and let out little squeals of admiration.
I remembered that this was a SECRET place that NOBODY ELSE knew about, and I felt myself drifting—consciousness and body separating, a floating, weightless sensation.
It was a sensation like carsickness—none of the pleasant warmth of sweet sake.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Just nausea.
My brain was probably flooding itself with chemicals to escape the reality that I was trapped here with this woman.
...But no matter how hard my brain tried to look away, the reality of her standing right in front of me wouldn't change...!
``Oh, right—Reina-chan.``
``...What is it?``
``...Are you okay?@k@vS09/20/172100107. You don't look well.``
``No... I'm fine.``
I drew in fresh air and dragged my consciousness back to reality.
...The nausea had passed, but my equilibrium was still off. I had to lean against a piece of scrap to keep my balance.
``I like you, Reina-chan.@k@vS09/20/172100109. Do you like me?``
``Ahahahaha... what's that, all of a sudden?``
I said that because I didn't want to say I liked her.
``I've been seeing your father for quite a while now, and... we've been talking about a lot of things.``
...How long was I going to keep drifting? Snap out of it, Rena.@k I'd heard something like this before.
The part of me trying to understand what this woman was about to say warred with the part refusing to.
Her words ricocheted inside my skull, reverberating.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ``We had a serious talk about our future.@k@vS09/20/172100112. About our life together, and all sorts of things.``
...What?
``You like Uncle Akihito, don't you, Reina?@k@vS09/00/173000016. So, Mommy is—``
...What was this?
...Mother?
What are you dredging up, Rena Ryuugu?!
At this critical moment, facing Rina-san one-on-one...!!
No, Mom, please—what about Dad?@k What about me?@k No divorce, no remarriage.@k ...Aaahh, no divorce, I won't allow it.@k No remarriage—
``I will never allow you to remarry my father.``
``I don't care how you see him, but... I will never allow you to marry him.``
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: She clearly hadn't expected me to say it this bluntly.
For a while, she seemed stunned.
...Eventually, she broke the silence with her own laughter.
``...Ah... ahahaha!@k@vS09/20/172100116. ...I figured rejection was a possibility, but I didn't think you'd come right out and say it like that... ahaha.``
``You expected I might reject you?@k@vS09/02/170200331. I'm surprised you even noticed I was pushing you away.``
``Of course I noticed.@k@vS09/20/172100118. You ran away every time you saw me.@k@vS09/20/172100119. Did you really think I hadn't caught on?@k@vS09/20/172100120. God, I hate brats.``
Her face twisted with ugly emotion.
...It should have been the first time she'd shown me that expression, yet I wasn't surprised at all.@k Because to me, her face had always looked exactly like that.

``I'd wanted to at least keep up appearances and get along with you, but it's hard when you hate me this much.@k@vS09/20/172100122. ...So what is it you don't like about me?@k@vS09/20/172100123. Tell me. For future reference.``
``Everything. From the start.@k@vS09/02/170200333. All of it.@k@vS09/02/170200334. I hate your smell, too.``
Venomous, aggressive words poured from my mouth one after another.
...A strange, restless exhilaration, as if I were soaring skyward without wings.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: A bizarre sensation, as if surrendering my body to a different me.@k I was standing in the middle of a critical showdown, yet it felt like someone else's business.

``Well, how convenient.@k@vS09/20/172100125. I hated you too, so I guess we're even.@k@vS09/20/172100126. Kyahahahahaha!@k@vS09/20/172100127. Don't fuck with me, you little shit—you want me to make you scream?``
``Don't ever come to our house again.@k@vS09/02/170200336. I will never allow you to marry my father.``

``Kyahahahahahaha!@k@vS09/20/172100129. I don't need your permission.@k@vS09/20/172100130. Go ahead—try to talk Daddy out of it. Let's see how that goes.@k@vS09/20/172100131. Not that it'd make any difference at this point.``
...Even after butting heads this directly, her brazen fearlessness gave me pause.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Was she that confident in her absolute hold over Dad?
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ...No—mere confidence wouldn't produce this level of bravado.@k She had something concrete to back it up...
Before I could ask, she told me herself.
``He proposed to me.``
``THAT'S A LIE!!!!!!``
``I-... It's not a lie.@k@vS09/20/172100134. It's true.``
A lie, a lie, a lie... the proposal was a complete lie...!!
Dad proposed? To this woman?? It was a lie—a bald-faced lie...!!
``He asked me, so it would be rude to refuse.@k@vS09/20/172100136. He even gave me a ring.@k@vS09/20/172100137. Besides, we'd been dating with marriage in mind.``
``If he tried to back out now after giving me a ring... well, that causes all sorts of legal problems nowadays, doesn't it?``

``That was your plan all along!!!@k@vS09/02/170200339. I heard everything!@k@vS09/02/170200340. I overheard you and that man Teppei talking at the coffee shop!!@k@vS09/02/170200341. I know you only went after Dad for his money!!``
``I know you call him your 'husband in Hinamizawa'! I know he's just a mark to you!@k@vS09/02/170200343. Kasai-san told me everything.@k@vS09/02/170200344. I know you're a con artist!!!``
``...Oh, you knew?@k@vS09/20/172100140. Well, well, well.``
Before I knew it, Rina was standing right in front of me.@k We glared at each other from close enough to bite each other's noses off.
``So?@k@vS09/20/172100142. Reina-chan—you know all this, and what exactly are you planning to do about it?``
``...Don't ever come near my father again...!``
``What if I say no?``

``You brought me to your secret hideout for this little chat, didn't you?@k@vS09/20/172100145. I figured this was what it'd be about.@k@vS09/20/172100146. After all, this is your secret hideout, right?@k@vS09/20/172100147. A secret place no one knows about, where no one can hear anything, right?``
``Yes, that's right.@k@vS09/02/170200348. ...No one knows this place, and no one can hear us.@k@vS09/02/170200349. ...A forgotten place no one ever visits...``
...Rena and Rina's faces drew close enough to bite each other's noses off.
Rena was dimly aware of what she was saying.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ...But her thoughts couldn't reach far enough to grasp what might happen next.
Having come this far... she understood, rationally, that there was no turning back.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ...But because there was no turning back, she didn't know what to do. She didn't know.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ``Kyahahahaha!@k@vS09/20/172100149. Oh, come on. Why so serious?@k@vS09/20/172100150. Here, let's make up~``
Rina suddenly burst out laughing and smacked Rena on the back.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ...Rena's expression didn't soften; she kept staring at Rina.@k But even when Rina draped both arms around Rena's neck with sickening familiarity, she felt no alarm.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: The tissue-thin veneer of tension tore, and Rina clamped down on Rena's throat as if to wring it apart!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Despite all the open hostility, Rena hadn't sensed this direct, murderous malice until the hands were already around her neck.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ``You smug little brat!!@k@vS09/20/172100153. I'm this close to rolling in tens of millions of yen!@k@vS09/20/172100154. If you'd at least played nice, I'd have tossed you some pocket money!!``
``But you just had to run your mouth—you stupid little bitch!!@k@vS09/20/172100156. Just fucking die!!!``
Rena clawed at the strangling hands with trembling fingers, but she couldn't muster the strength to pry them free.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ``I didn't want to kill you, you know!@k@vS09/20/172100159. I'll probably never find another mark this rich!!@k@vS09/20/172100160. But strangling you for tens of millions? Not a bad trade!``
``I was going to vanish after taking the money anyway!@k@vS09/20/172100162. This is what you get for mouthing off!!!``
My vision rapidly darkened to a murky red-black.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...This was the first time I'd ever faced genuine murderous intent.@k It didn't frighten me.@k ...Because fear, in the end, is a feeling people use when watching a disaster from the safety of the far shore.
When your life is on the line, there is no fear.@k If I loosened my grip even slightly, she would crush my throat. A raw, simple contest of force.
Rina seemed to be trying to push me down and mount me to secure total dominance.
If I allowed that, any chance of fighting back would be gone.
I managed to hold our positions, but being choked drained me far faster—"equal" wasn't remotely the right word.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Rena deliberately broke the stalemate.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: She purposely fell backward, trying to throw Rina off balance.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Staying locked in this contest of strength would only mean suffocating.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: So she had no choice but to gamble on this slender chance!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Resistance suddenly vanished, and the two tumbled to the ground in a tangle.
But Rina's killing intent was absolute; a mere fall wasn't enough to make her release Rena's throat.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Even so, for one fleeting instant, Rina's grip loosened.
Not enough to break free—but enough to buy Rena a precious moment where her hands didn't need to fight the stranglehold.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: If she failed to seize that moment, it was over.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: So Rena literally clawed at the ground, trying to grasp her chance.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Clawing, scrabbling, grasping!
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Rena's right hand struck a shard of glass.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: It was truly, literally, a chance to snatch life from the jaws of death.
She drove the glass shard into the soft inner side of Rina's wrist!!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ``...GYAAAAAAHHHH!!!``
Rina tried to endure the searing pain for a few heartbeats.
But she couldn't withstand the force of a cornered rat driving the shard deeper.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: She released Rena's throat, clutching her blood-gushing wrist.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Seizing the opening, Rena rolled sideways and finally escaped.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ``...ggh, ggggh...!!!@k@vS09/20/172100165. It fucking HURTS...!!``
Pain-wracked groans mixed with attempt at intimidation.
But intimidation was meaningless now.@k Intimidation works by exploiting an opponent's reluctance to fight.
In the midst of actual combat, screaming only advertised how badly you were wounded!
Rena no longer had any hesitation.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: She'd just learned from Rina herself.@k What you do in a moment like this!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: This was Rena's secret hideout.@k There was nothing here she didn't know!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Had Rena known, somewhere deep inside, that a day like this would come?
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: Or was it truly just coincidence that it was there?@k Rena found a lead pipe, about a meter long, as though it were the most natural thing in the world.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: She raised it high and brought it crashing down!!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: No one could block this heavy, hard lead pipe with their arms.
Perhaps Rina had tried to catch it in her palms and wrench it away.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: But her long nails betrayed her.
Her fingers buckled on impact, several breaking, nails tearing off.
``GYAAAH!!!``
That scream told Rena the advantage had shifted.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: She had to press it now!!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: If she didn't, this slim advantage would be reversed in an instant—just as she herself had reversed it moments ago—and this time she would be the one strangled to death!!
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ``Gya!! Ugg!! Wai—! Time out—time out!!!``
Rina shielded her head with both arms, but it was pointless.@k It just meant her arms would shatter before her skull.
``I-I'm serious, this isn't funny...!! Ghak!! Gya!!``
This isn't a joke.
Die, die, die.@k@|@y Just die...!
Yes. That's right.
This is what I should have done.
Evidence? Consulting someone?@k@r@|@y All pointless, all meaningless,@k@r@|@y truly, utterly meaningless!!
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I should have done this from the start!!
If I'd done this sooner—!!
Dad would never have been ensnared.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I could have protected Dad.
I could have protected my family.
I could have protected my life.
I could have remained myself.
Mom would never have divorced.
And I would have been happy all along.
I would never have had to be unhappy.
But I will take back my happiness.
Right now, this moment, with my own strength.
I will not weep over misfortune.
I will not surrender to an unhappy fate.
I will be happy.
I will seize it—
Now!
With my own—
HANDS!!!!
Rina tried to flee several times.
But with both hands useless and the treacherous footing of the junk heap beneath her, escape was impossible.
Yes.@k This was my hideout.
My domain.@k ...There was no escaping me here.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: She tried to scramble up the slope of scrap, slipped, fell, and rolled away into the distance.
After that, she didn't get up, didn't shield her head. Her eyes remained open, her neck bent at an unnatural angle.
...I didn't know how to confirm death, so I stood watching her open, unblinking eyes for a while.@k Suspicious that she might be feigning.
But no matter how long I waited, her eyelids never closed.
I scooped up a handful of sand and flung it at her face.@k Still, Rina's eyes didn't close.
Thud.@k ...Slick with sweat, the lead pipe slipped from my hand.
When I tried to pick it up, I was startled by how heavy it was—had I really been swinging something this heavy so effortlessly?
My entire body was drenched in sweat, and the cooling wind felt wonderful.
The chorus of higurashi soothed my electrified heart.
Rina was no different from a broken mannequin now.@k ...Yes—she was junk.
I, the sovereign of this junkyard kingdom, had bestowed upon her a form befitting its residents.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I felt no guilt whatsoever about having killed a person.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: The sense of accomplishment from having vanquished the evil that had seduced my father far outweighed any such feeling.
As my ragged breathing calmed, my ability to coolly analyze the situation returned.
...I would hide Rina's body.
First, I had to hide her scooter.
I climbed the slope and pushed the scooter parked on the embankment down the incline.
It tumbled down with heavy thuds, coming to rest amid the debris as if it had always been there.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I hadn't lured her here to kill her.
...Or had I? Perhaps some part of me, deep down, had intended this all along when I led her here.
But in hindsight, this had been the fastest, best solution.
There was no better answer than killing her.@k How pathetic, those days I'd spent moping and hoping someone else would fix things.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: ...I thought of last year—the murder of Satoshi-kun's aunt.
I'd never doubted that Satoshi-kun was the true culprit,@k but today, standing here, I understood more completely than ever before.
In that situation, at that time.@k How else could he possibly have saved Satoko-chan, if not by killing the aunt?
My case was no different.
How else could I have saved Dad, if not by killing her?
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: If I'd tried to plan a murder deliberately, the pressure would have been crushing.
...But, for better or for worse, the moment came upon me suddenly.
And so.@k I must have resolved my problem in the way that put the least burden on myself.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...If this woman vanished after today, would anyone grow suspicious?
She was an irresponsible woman who skipped work on a whim.
No one would question it if she simply disappeared one day.
changeReason: Narrative perspective changed from first person to third person

TextENGNew: She'd been shady from the start.
Everyone would assume she'd gotten entangled in some mess and gone underground.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: I didn't need any fancy alibi.
If I could just make her body disappear, everything would be erased completely.
I spent a moment considering how to handle that final step.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Because every method of hiding a body carried considerable risk.
...But my restored composure produced an answer quickly.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Yes... this place was safest.@k A forgotten place.
No one comes here.
No one knows about it.
I knew that, because I'd spent so much time here.
Rather than risk transporting the body elsewhere, hiding it here was far safer.
But even that wasn't perfect.
This place wouldn't remain secret forever.
...I would hide the body here for now, and in the meantime, find a way to erase it from the face of the earth.
Thinking about that was infinitely easier than the agonizing I'd done over how to separate Rina from Dad.
I stuffed the corpse into a broken refrigerator among the trash.
I considered burying the refrigerator under dirt and debris.
...But that was too simple.@k I would need to dismember the corpse and dispose of the pieces completely.
I could hear distant thunder hinting at an evening storm.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: Rain would be very convenient.
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: It would wash away whatever traces of Rina's filthy blood might remain.
I should go home.@k And think about tomorrow.
I would stay by Dad's side until he forgot about that woman.
...He might be hurt at first.
But... we would reclaim our happiness.@k A happy, peaceful life.
My body was leaden with exhaustion.
But I also felt the accomplishment of having taken a crucial first step.
I would keep taking step after step.
I will not cry.
I will not cry until I have won back my happiness with my own hands.
I swear it.
